Description: Three-layer architecture with MVVM presentation. Domain layer has no framework dependencies
When To Use: Large production apps; teams > 3 developers; apps needing high testability; long-lived projects
When Not To Use: Prototypes; solo projects; hackathons; apps with < 5 screens
Structure: lib/ core/ constants/ theme/ utils/ errors/ features/ auth/ data/ datasources/ models/ repositories/ domain/ entities/ repositories/ usecases/ presentation/ bloc/ pages/ widgets/ home/ data/ domain/ presentation/ shared/ widgets/ services/
Key Principles: Dependency inversion; domain has no framework deps; use cases encapsulate business rules; repository abstracts data source

Description: Organize code by feature/module with each feature self-contained
When To Use: Medium to large apps; multiple teams working in parallel; when features can be developed independently
When Not To Use: Small apps where layered is simpler; apps with heavily shared logic between features
Structure: lib/ features/ auth/ data/ domain/ presentation/ products/ data/ domain/ presentation/ cart/ data/ domain/ presentation/ checkout/ core/ di/ network/ storage/ shared/ widgets/ l10n/ theme/
Key Principles: Each feature is self-contained; shared code in core/shared; features communicate via interfaces; easy to add/remove features

Description: Traditional MVC pattern adapted for mobile. Controller manages state and business logic
When To Use: Simple apps; learning projects; apps with straightforward CRUD operations
When Not To Use: Complex apps with rich business logic; apps needing extensive testing; large teams
Structure: lib/ models/ user.dart product.dart views/ home_view.dart profile_view.dart controllers/ home_controller.dart profile_controller.dart services/ api_service.dart widgets/ common/
Key Principles: Simple structure; fast to develop; controller handles both business logic and state; suitable for small-medium apps

Description: Event-driven architecture using BLoC pattern for state management across the app
When To Use: Flutter apps with complex state management; when clear separation of UI and logic is needed
When Not To Use: Simple apps; when BLoC verbosity is not justified
Structure: lib/ blocs/ auth/ auth_bloc.dart auth_event.dart auth_state.dart products/ cart/ data/ repositories/ models/ screens/ home/ widgets/ services/
Key Principles: Events in, states out; BLoC is testable without Flutter; one BLoC per feature/screen; use Cubit for simpler cases

Description: Provider-based architecture with compile-time safety and auto-disposal
When To Use: Flutter apps wanting minimal boilerplate; when dependency injection integrated with state management is needed
When Not To Use: Legacy apps already using another state management; strongly typed event-driven requirements
Structure: lib/ providers/ auth_provider.dart products_provider.dart cart_provider.dart models/ repositories/ screens/ home/ product_detail/ cart/ widgets/ services/
Key Principles: Providers are global but auto-disposed; ProviderScope for DI; ref.watch for reactive; ref.read for one-shot

Description: Rich domain model with aggregates entities and value objects driving the architecture
When To Use: Complex business domains (fintech healthcare e-commerce); apps with complex validation rules
When Not To Use: Simple CRUD apps; apps with thin business logic; prototypes
Structure: lib/ domain/ entities/ value_objects/ repositories/ failures/ events/ application/ bloc/ usecases/ infrastructure/ datasources/ dtos/ repositories/ presentation/ pages/ widgets/ core/ injection.dart
Key Principles: Ubiquitous language; aggregates enforce invariants; value objects are immutable; domain events for side effects

Description: App shell with dynamically loadable feature modules or mini-apps
When To Use: Super apps (WeChat-like); apps with pluggable features; large orgs with separate teams per feature
When Not To Use: Simple single-purpose apps; apps with few features
Structure: app_shell/ core/ navigation/ auth/ features/ (separate packages) feature_payments/ lib/ test/ pubspec.yaml feature_social/ lib/ test/ pubspec.yaml feature_marketplace/ lib/ test/ pubspec.yaml
Key Principles: Feature isolation via packages; shared contracts in core; lazy loading of features; independent deployment possible

Description: Local database as primary data store with background sync to server
When To Use: Apps used in areas with poor connectivity; field service apps; note-taking apps
When Not To Use: Apps requiring real-time data; apps where offline makes no sense (live streaming)
Structure: lib/ data/ local/ database/ dao/ remote/ api/ sync/ sync_service.dart conflict_resolver.dart domain/ models/ repositories/ presentation/ services/ connectivity_service.dart
Key Principles: Local DB is source of truth; sync queue for pending changes; conflict resolution strategy; connectivity monitoring

Description: Multiple packages in one repository managed by Melos for code sharing
When To Use: Multiple apps sharing code (customer + driver app); shared design system across apps
When Not To Use: Single app with no shared code; small team with one product
Structure: packages/ shared_ui/ lib/ widgets/ themes/ pubspec.yaml core/ lib/ models/ services/ pubspec.yaml app_customer/ lib/ pubspec.yaml app_driver/ lib/ pubspec.yaml melos.yaml
Key Principles: Shared packages for common code; independent versioning; parallel development; unified CI/CD

Description: UI structure and content defined by server responses enabling updates without app releases
When To Use: Content-heavy apps; apps needing frequent UI changes without releases; A/B testing
When Not To Use: Apps with static UI; performance-critical rendering; complex animations
Structure: lib/ sdui/ widgets/ widget_factory.dart renderers/ parsers/ json_parser.dart screens/ dynamic_screen.dart data/ sdui_repository.dart
Key Principles: Server sends UI schema as JSON; client renders dynamically; fallback for unknown components; cache schemas for offline
